Flight Management Systems.clue: This is the main service an FMS delivers to the flight crew during normal operations.
choices: Automated navigation and performance computations
Ground proximity and traffic collision alerting
Automatic engine start and FADEC sequencing
Direct manual reversion of flight controls
Flight Management Systems.explanation: The FMS automates navigation and performance; alerting, engine starts, and manual reversion belong to other systems.

Flight Management Systems.clue: These four sensor sources are blended by the FMC to produce a fused aircraft position.
choices: Magnetometer and TCAS traffic processor
GPS, IRS, ADC, and VOR/DME
Weather radar feed and transponder reply
Pitot probe and radar altimeter pair
Flight Management Systems.explanation: The FMC fuses satellite, inertial, air data, and ground-based radio navigation inputs.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This set of LRUs forms the architecture of a conventional FMS installation.
choices: FDR, CVR, and ELT recorder bundle
AHRS, ADC, and audio control panel units
CDU, FMC, navigation database, and sensors
MFD, VHF radio, and TAWS processor
Flight Management Systems.explanation: The FMS is defined by CDU entry, FMC computation, nav database storage, and sensor inputs.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This is where a published STAR procedure physically lives inside the avionics suite.
choices: An ADS-B ground station database
A maintenance laptop storage drive
The aircraft configuration module
The FMS navigation database
Flight Management Systems.explanation: STARs, SIDs, and approaches are stored in the FMS nav database on a 28-day cycle.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This is the interface parameter that technicians must verify during FMS installation to ensure LRUs talk correctly.
choices: ARINC 429 label mapping between LRUs
ADF loop antenna orientation angle
Cabin reading light dimmer setting
Intercom squelch threshold level
Flight Management Systems.explanation: Label mapping determines which data words each LRU exchanges with the FMS.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This digital data bus standard carries FMC output to cockpit display units.
choices: RS-232 serial interface
ARINC 429 digital data bus
MIL-STD-1553 command bus
Controller area network bus
Flight Management Systems.explanation: ARINC 429 is the one-way civil aviation bus that dominates FMC-to-EFIS paths.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This is the device pilots use to type waypoints and flight plan data into the FMS.
choices: The transponder code entry pad
The audio control panel selector
The CDU keyboard
The EFIS range and mode controller
Flight Management Systems.explanation: The Control Display Unit keyboard is the primary pilot interface for FMS data entry.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This is the coupling capability a technician must confirm when tying the FMS into the autopilot.
choices: Weather radar overlay on the display
Autobrake arming and disarm logic
ADS-B transmit and receive function
Lateral and vertical guidance coupling
Flight Management Systems.explanation: Both LNAV and VNAV must command the autopilot correctly after FMS integration.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This is the test tool used to decode ARINC 429 data from an FMC.
choices: A dedicated data bus protocol analyzer
A digital multimeter with frequency counter
A portable pitot-static test set
A fiber optic power level meter
Flight Management Systems.explanation: Only a protocol analyzer can parse ARINC 429 labels and SSM bits.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This is the FMC output a technician should inspect when LNAV is programmed correctly but the autopilot refuses to capture the route.
choices: VOR receiver signal strength indication
ARINC 429 roll steering output to the autopilot
Pitot-static system leak test results
Audio panel transmit and receive routing
Flight Management Systems.explanation: Roll steering is the lateral command bus from FMC to autopilot.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This is the test that proves the FMS is accepting valid GPS position data.
choices: An audio sidetone level measurement
A VHF standing wave ratio check
A sensor data validity and alignment test
A DC bus voltage under load test
Flight Management Systems.explanation: A validity/alignment test verifies the FMS is accepting the GPS position.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This change occurs in the active flight plan when a direct-to clearance is entered on the CDU.
choices: The FMS drops into dead reckoning mode
VNAV is disabled for the remainder of the flight
The autopilot disconnects for manual flight
The active leg shifts to direct to the selected waypoint
Flight Management Systems.explanation: Direct-to simply retargets the active leg; other modes keep working.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This installation error best explains why LNAV is failing while VNAV works normally on a newly installed FMS.
choices: Roll steering wiring or label assignment error
Vertical gyro power supply connection issue
Pitot-static plumbing to ADC inputs
GPS antenna coaxial cable routing error
Flight Management Systems.explanation: If VNAV works, air data and GPS are fine; the issue is on the lateral steering path.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This is the usual reason a correctly identified airport offers no selectable SID in the FMS.
choices: The GPS receiver has insufficient satellite coverage
The navigation database is outdated or incomplete
The CDU display brightness is set too low
The FMS software is stuck in maintenance mode
Flight Management Systems.explanation: SID availability depends on nav database currency and coverage.

Flight Management Systems.clue: These are the first inputs to verify when an FMC POSITION INVALID alert appears on the CDU at power-up.
choices: Fuel quantity probe wiring continuity
Replace the CDU with a known good unit
GPS and IRS data reaching the FMC
Audio panel RF emissions from the headset bus
Flight Management Systems.explanation: Position validity depends on GPS and IRS inputs to the FMC.

Flight Management Systems.clue: This condition during a battery replacement explains why stored FMS flight plans vanish.
choices: Pitot-static system needed recalibration
VHF NAV radio was left in self-test mode
Fuel quantity computer lost its calibration
FMS memory lost backup power during the swap
Flight Management Systems.explanation: Volatile FMS memory clears when power is lost during a battery change.

Flight Management Systems.clue: These system inputs should be verified when VNAV descent profiles are inaccurate after loading a STAR.
choices: ADC pressure data and performance initialization
HF radio antenna tuner settings
Passenger address amplifier output level
EGPWS terrain database currency
Flight Management Systems.explanation: VNAV relies on air data and performance data to compute the descent path.

Flight Management Systems.clue: When the flight crew wants an automatic top-of-descent calculation to meet a crossing altitude, they engage this FMS mode.
choices: Lateral navigation mode
Vertical navigation mode
Direct-to navigation mode
Holding pattern mode
Flight Management Systems.explanation: VNAV is the dedicated vertical path optimizer in the FMS.
